primary_concern: Sepsis risk with concurrent acute kidney injury (AKI) and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (WBC 14.2 K/uL), anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL), and thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) suggests possible hepatic involvement. AKI progression from Stage 1 (Cr 2.1 mg/dL) to Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL [excluded outlier]) indicates severe renal dysfunction. Thrombocytopenia (<100 K/uL) meets SOFA coagulation scoring criteria.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria (suspected infection + SOFA increase ≥2)
Creatinine elevation meets KDIGO Stage 3 criteria (≥3x baseline) despite outlier exclusion
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5x baseline in 7d

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulopathy scoring (threshold: <150 K/uL)

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per sepsis protocol
Order blood cultures and infectious workup
Assess for source of infection (urinary, respiratory, or abdominal)
Monitor AKI progression with hourly urine output and fluid balance
Consider platelet transfusion if <50 K/uL or active bleeding
Recheck lactate trend and arterial blood gas
doctor_handoff: Review sepsis source control options, confirm AKI etiology (pre-renal vs intrinsic), and assess need for nephrology consultation. Verify platelet function tests and coagulation panel. Re-evaluate creatinine trajectory considering excluded outlier.

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2, Hgb 9.8, Bilirubin 1.4
AKI Stage 1 (Cr 2.1) with platelet decline to 95 K/uL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
